political.s: H.B. Fuller reported minimal political contributions of $22,300 in 2024 and no federal lobbying activity. The company does not demonstrate a clear partisan lean, with modest campaign finance activity distributed relatively neutrally.

charity.s: H.B. Fuller Company Foundation donated more than $1.3 million to over 300 organizations in 2024. The foundation focuses on STEM education and youth leadership development, with employees volunteering over 6,600 hours across 21 countries. Community impact initiatives emphasize education equity and leadership development.
charity.focus: STEM education, youth leadership development, community engagement

environment.s: H.B. Fuller reports steady progress toward sustainability targets including reducing energy and GHG emissions intensity by 20% and waste/water intensity by 10% by 2025. The company has over 50% of new product portfolio focused on sustainable solutions and is working toward Science Based Targets Initiative (SBTi) standards, with ISO 14001 certifications across multiple sites.

labor.s: H.B. Fuller's safety record is nearly three times better than industry average TRIR and five times better on lost-time rates. However, an NLRB case involving bad faith bargaining (Case 10-CA-341238) and historical labor concerns suggest mixed labor practices. The company offers competitive compensation and employee development programs including mentorship and the Talent Up Program.

dei.s: H.B. Fuller explicitly identifies diversity and inclusion as a priority ESG material issue. The company operates three Employee Network Groups (BEN, NextGen Pros, W@F) with nearly 1,000 members across North America and Latin America. Board composition includes 50% female representation and 88% independent directors, reflecting commitment to inclusive leadership.

animals.s: N/A - H.B. Fuller manufactures adhesives, sealants, and specialty chemicals. The company does not conduct animal testing and does not test on animals as part of its adhesive/chemical manufacturing operations.

guns.s: N/A - H.B. Fuller is an adhesives and specialty chemicals manufacturer. The company neither manufactures nor sells firearms or firearm-related products.

privacy.s: H.B. Fuller maintains privacy policies consistent with global business standards and commits to treating data with respect without third-party sharing. Limited public disclosure of specific data privacy practices, security standards, or third-party audits is available.

execPay.s: Previous CEO James Owens earned $10.3 million in his final year (2022). Current CEO Celeste Mastin received a $1.7 million signing bonus and $400,774 in relocation expenses when hired as COO. Average executive compensation is approximately $231,834 annually, though specific pay ratios are not clearly disclosed.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
